items.content: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

items.title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake leaves dozens dead in the Philippines
items.standfirst: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines off the island of Mindanao, killing at least 32.
items.content: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines off the island of Mindanao, killing at least 32.

items.title: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 35 dead
items.standfirst: People told not to enter damaged buildings for fear of aftershocks from magnitude-7.8 quakeAt least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts.The quake hit early in the morning about 20km (12.4 miles) off the coast of Sarangani province, with tremors felt strongly across Mindanao and 420km away in the city of Manado on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. Continue reading...

items.content: A deadly earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing dozens of people and sending small tsunami waves toward at least three nations.

clusters.title: Israel and Iran step back from renewed conflict after Trump calls for halt
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel and Iran step back from renewed conflict after Trump calls for halt
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Israel and Iran step back from renewed conflict after Trump calls for halt
items.standfirst: Netanyahu acknowledges pause in fighting in TV speech but vows forceful response to future attacksMiddle East crisis – live updatesFears of a return to a full-scale regional war in the Middle East eased on Monday as Israel and Iran said they had halted attacks on each other after an appeal from Donald Trump to “immediately stop shooting”.Benjamin Netanyahu, Israel’s prime minister, acknowledged the halt in fighting with Iran in a televised speech, but vowed to respond “with force” to future attacks. Continue reading...
items.content: Netanyahu acknowledges pause in fighting in TV speech but vows forceful response to future attacksMiddle East crisis – live updatesFears of a return to a full-scale regional war in the Middle East eased on Monday as Israel and Iran said they had halted attacks on each other after an appeal from Donald Trump to “immediately stop shooting”.Benjamin Netanyahu, Israel’s prime minister, acknowledged the halt in fighting with Iran in a televised speech, but vowed to respond “with force” to future attacks. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
items.standfirst: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.
items.content: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

items.title: Trump tells Israel and Iran to stop shooting, both sides say attacks halted after weekend flare-up
items.standfirst: President Trump told Israel and Iran to "immediately stop 'shooting'" at each other and said both sides were seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after the weekend flare-up that began with Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon's capital Beirut. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Nancy Cordes have more.
items.content: President Trump told Israel and Iran to "immediately stop 'shooting'" at each other and said both sides were seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after the weekend flare-up that began with Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon's capital Beirut. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Nancy Cordes have more.

items.title: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes after first exchange of fire since truce
items.standfirst: Iran launched about 30 missiles at Israel following a strike in Lebanon, while Israel carried out two waves of air strikes in Iran.
items.content: Iran launched about 30 missiles at Israel following a strike in Lebanon, while Israel carried out two waves of air strikes in Iran.

items.title: Iran war: who is fighting and why?
items.standfirst: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...
items.content: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel shuts down following war flare-up with Iran
items.standfirst: Iran and Israel say they've now halted attacks, after their first exchange of fire since a ceasefire in April. US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back. Among ordinary Israelis, it stoked of a return to full-blown war.
items.content: Iran and Israel say they've now halted attacks, after their first exchange of fire since a ceasefire in April. US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back. Among ordinary Israelis, it stoked of a return to full-blown war.

items.title: Iranians feel ‘sense of pride’ after launching strikes on Israel
items.standfirst: Iranians took to the streets of Tehran after Iran launched missiles toward Israel for the first time since a ceasefire was reached in April. The strikes came after Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs in retaliation for alleged Hezbollah attacks.
items.content: Iranians took to the streets of Tehran after Iran launched missiles toward Israel for the first time since a ceasefire was reached in April. The strikes came after Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs in retaliation for alleged Hezbollah attacks.

clusters.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
items.standfirst: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.
items.content: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.

items.title: Trump walks out of interview with NBC
items.standfirst: President Trump abruptly ended an interview with NBC News when repeatedly challenged on his claims of election fraud
items.content: President Trump abruptly ended an interview with NBC News when repeatedly challenged on his claims of election fraud

items.title: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
items.standfirst: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.
items.content: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

items.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker
items.standfirst: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.
items.content: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

items.title: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un met on Monday at a rare summit in Pyongyang.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un met on Monday at a rare summit in Pyongyang.

items.title: China's Xi Jinping makes rare visit to North Korea
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping received a red carpet welcome on a rare visit to North Korea ahead of a two-day summit. CBS News' Anna Coren has more.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping received a red carpet welcome on a rare visit to North Korea ahead of a two-day summit. CBS News' Anna Coren has more.

items.title: ‘A special sense of closeness’: China’s Xi vows stronger ties with North Korea in rare visit
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Monday said that China world not swerve from its commitment ​to safeguarding common interests with North Korea or waver in its support for Kim Jong Un during a rare summit between the two leaders in Pyongyang. The summit comes at a time when China's economy, strengthened by growing trade and military ties to Russia, could boost ⁠North Korea's confidence in talks.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Monday said that China world not swerve from its commitment ​to safeguarding common interests with North Korea or waver in its support for Kim Jong Un during a rare summit between the two leaders in Pyongyang. The summit comes at a time when China's economy, strengthened by growing trade and military ties to Russia, could boost ⁠North Korea's confidence in talks.

items.title: Kim Jong Un hosts Xi Jinping from a position of rare strength
items.standfirst: Kim Jong Un offered China’s president a grand welcome Monday.
items.content: Kim Jong Un offered China’s president a grand welcome Monday.

clusters.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
clusters.neutral_headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
items.standfirst: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.
items.content: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.

items.title: Armenian election largely seen as 'national referendum on country's direction'
items.standfirst: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party won parliamentary elections, according to preliminary results on June 8. The election signals a victory endorsing the nation's pro-Western shift after threats from Moscow and claims of Russian interference. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot tells us more from Yerevan.
items.content: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party won parliamentary elections, according to preliminary results on June 8. The election signals a victory endorsing the nation's pro-Western shift after threats from Moscow and claims of Russian interference. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot tells us more from Yerevan.

items.title: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.
items.content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.

items.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election and cements shift away from Russia
items.standfirst: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...

clusters.title: NATO jets shoot down drone over Latvia, extending Ukraine spillover fears
clusters.neutral_headline: NATO jets shoot down drone over Latvia, extending Ukraine spillover fears

items.title: NATO jets shoot down drone over Latvia, extending Ukraine spillover fears
items.standfirst: The drone entered Latvian airspace due to 'Russian electronic warfare', the military says.
items.content: The drone entered Latvian airspace due to 'Russian electronic warfare', the military says.

items.title: French NATO jet shoots down drone over Latvia as another explodes in Moldova
items.standfirst: A drone that entered NATO member Latvia's airspace from Russia on Monday was shot down by a French military fighter jet, as fragments of another drone were found in a field in Moldova after it entered from Ukraine earlier the same day. Military drones straying into the airspace of ​Russia's neighbours have stoked concerns that the war in Ukraine is spilling over NATO's borders.
items.content: A drone that entered NATO member Latvia's airspace from Russia on Monday was shot down by a French military fighter jet, as fragments of another drone were found in a field in Moldova after it entered from Ukraine earlier the same day. Military drones straying into the airspace of ​Russia's neighbours have stoked concerns that the war in Ukraine is spilling over NATO's borders.

items.title: ‘Senior lieutenant’ in Kinahan criminal cartel jailed for 24 years by Dublin court
items.standfirst: Sean McGovern pleaded guilty to two charges relating to a deadly feud after being extradited from the UAEA leader of the notorious Kinahan criminal cartel has been sentenced to 24 years in prison at a Dublin court.Sean McGovern, 40, who has been described as a senior lieutenant in the group, pleaded guilty to two charges of directing the activities of a criminal organisation relating to a deadly feud between the Kinahan and Hutch criminal gangs. Continue reading...
